steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined sentence is: "Such analysis, in turn, can contribute to reports of exaggerated effects." The phrase in turn signals a chain of consequences. The preceding sentence states that small sample sizes increase the potential for biased analysis. The underlined sentence names what biased analysis can then lead to. The question asks what function this sentence serves in the passage.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It summarizes a shift in scientists' understanding…': the sentence says nothing about a shift in understanding of how the fish responded to ocean acidification. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It emphasizes the magnitude of the effect reported by Allan and colleagues…': the sentence is about what biased analysis produces, not about restating Allan et al.'s original findings. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It counters the objection of Clark and colleagues…': the sentence actually extends Clark et al.'s point, not opposes it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It elaborates on a potential consequence of Allan and colleagues' reliance on a relatively small sample size': Clark et al. warned that small samples increase biased analysis; the underlined sentence adds that such analysis can then lead to exaggerated reports — a downstream consequence of the small sample size. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
